title: centaurea montana subsp. lingulata
intro: Centaurea montana subsp. lingulata, commonly known as mountain cornflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the aster family, characterized by its blue-violet flowers and hairy, green leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic activities, due to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. Consuming Centaurea montana subsp. lingulata is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of conditions, from digestive issues to respiratory problems.
